Petition For Statutory Attorney'S Fees And For Attorney'S Fees For Extraordinary Services
Off Calendar Aug-13-2013 Continued To Oct-02-13 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Bailey Penzotti for and with Toshia Webster (Petitioner/Objector). Ann Draper for and with Peter Clerides (Objector to Mr. Murphy's fees). Terrence Murphy in Propria Persona. Jonathan Ferdon for San Francisco Public Administrator.

Counsel presented oral argument to support their positions with regard to Mr. Murphy's request for extraordinary fees, statutory fees being split between Mr. Murphy and Mr. Clerides, Ms. Webster being required to file an accounting, removal of Toshia Webster, the Public Administrator being brought in, whether Ms. Webster engaged in fraud, ethical and professional code violations, etc. The petition for attorney's fees and the accounting petition were taken under submission.

Judge: John Dearman. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy T. Bradley. (Not Reported)

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Apr-30-2013 Continued To Jul-02-13 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Terrence Murphy in Propera Persona. Bailey Penzotti for and with Toshia Webster. Jonathan Ferdon for the San Francisco Public Administator.

The Court requested that the parties go into the hallway to meet and confer. Ms. Penzotti reported that discussion did not go well and stated that her client acknowledges that Mr. Ferdon is entitled to fees and proposes a payment schedule of $400.00 per month. Mr. Ferdon reports that his fees are at the discretion of the court and although he does not prefer to be on a payment plan, he does not wish that Toshia Webster have to sell the home to pay fees. Both Ms. Penzotti and Mr. Murphy presented oral argument to support their positions as to why their proposed allocation of statutory fees should be the course of action taken, explained the extent of services provided, calculation of time spent working on the case, and agreed that the retainer contracts that Toshia Webster signed was as an individual and not as the Executor of the Estate. The Court passes the matter at 10:05 a.m. At 10:40 a.m., the matter is recalled. The Court raises the issue that since the contract was in Toshia Webster's personal capacity, how could the Court make a ruling for fees out of Probate. Furthermore, the Court noted that Toshia Webster refinanced the property yet there is no mention of where the funds are and how they have been spent and the Court cannot determine if the estate has been harmed. Therefore, this matter is put over for an amended petition to be filed. Ms. Penzotti addressed the Francis Davis claim and explained that it has been rejected due to its untimely filing. Mr. Murphy presented his solution for a remedy that a Trust be set up to make sure fees are paid or the Public Guardian be reappointed to make distribution. Mr. Ferdon reported that the Public Guardian is not allowed back into the case until a proper accounting is done. Mr. Murphy requested a statement of decision if the matter is not resolved. The accounting petition and petition for statutory attorney's fees, etc. were continued to July 2,2013 at 10:30 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Court encouraged the parties to talk about resolution.
